text: In this sterile, modern environment, I feel at once like a participant and an observer. The glassed-in room suggests boundaries—between inside and outside, between those speaking and those watching. The R+D display calls attention to human innovation, to our collective urge to respond and develop. Yet, the subtle awkwardness in the clinical lighting, regimented chairs, and looping screens creates in me a sense of disconnect, as if I’m part of an experiment. I’m curious, intellectually engaged, but I also feel slightly unmoored—a visitor, contemplating what it means to connect within these structured spaces.
